reforms.description: The Czech Republic aims to enhance its workforce skills to address labour shortages and improve productivity, emphasizing the need for better skilling and innovation to maintain competitiveness in global value chains. The Ministry of Education, Youth and Sports has prepared an 'Action Plan for Development of Human Resources for Research, Development and Innovation and Gender Equality in Research, Development and Innovation for the years 2018-2020', which was approved by the Government and aims to guide educational strategies and workforce development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving workforce skills is expected to enhance productivity and innovation, which supports economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Action Plan was approved by the Government and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms are significant in addressing skill shortages and improving workforce capabilities but do not represent a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Education, Youth and Sports (MEYS) prepared 'Action Plan for Development of Human Resources for Research, Development and Innovation and Gender Equality in Research, Development and Innovation in the Czech Republic for the years 2018-2020'.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic is implementing the Industry 4.0 strategy, which focuses on upgrading data and information infrastructure to facilitate knowledge diffusion and technological change adoption. Additionally, a funding strategy is being developed for projects under this strategy to enhance productivity and innovation in manufacturing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Upgrading infrastructure and providing funding for innovation projects are expected to enhance productivity and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy is currently being implemented with ongoing developments.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Industry 4.0 strategy represents a major structural change in the approach to innovation and productivity in the Czech Republic.
reforms.source_quote: A key policy of the Industry 4.0 strategy is to upgrade data and information infrastructure to facilitate knowledge diffusion and adoption of technological change.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Education, Youth and Sports is implementing a call called 'Building Expert Capacities - Transfer of Technologies' aimed at supporting the development of centers for technology transfer at research organizations in the Czech Republic.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting technology transfer is expected to enhance commercialization of research outputs, thereby contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The call is currently being implemented to support technology transfer centers.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative is significant in enhancing technology transfer capabilities but does not represent a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: MEYS implemented within the OP RDE a call called 'Building Expert Capacities - Transfer of Technologies' aimed at supporting the development of centres and other necessary background for technology transfer.

reforms.description: A new data collection system for monitoring health care quality was established, allowing for the creation of a comprehensive National Registry of Reimbursed Health Services, which will enhance performance indicators and improve the quality of care.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving health care quality monitoring is expected to enhance overall health system performance, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The data collection system is currently in place and operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important for health care, this reform does not represent a major structural change in innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: The amendment permits to set up a comprehensive National Registry of Reimbursed Health Services.
